parameters.content:
## Notify sponsor or security

Validates whether a queued account has a usable sponsor email, then posts either a sponsor notification or an IT Security alert to Teams.

## Record deletion outcomes

Records successful deletions to the SharePoint audit list and marks pending records deleted, while failed deletions generate a Teams alert and mark the pending item as errored.

## Delete or retain account

Handles the closely placed deletion and retention outcomes: eligible guests are deleted in Entra ID and checked for success, while exception-matched records are marked retained and logged to Teams.

## Check retention exceptions

Looks up each overdue guest in the retention-exceptions list and branches based on whether deletion should be blocked.

## Collect overdue deletions

Gets overdue pending-deletion records from SharePoint, checks whether any are due, splits due records for item-by-item processing, or ends when nothing is due.

## Daily execution setup

Starts the daily deletion execution run and loads configuration for pending deletions, audit logging, and retention exceptions.

## Queue new candidates

Checks the SharePoint pending-deletions list to avoid duplicates, queues newly identified stale guests, and skips accounts that are already pending.

## Find stale guests

Retrieves guest users from Microsoft Graph and filters each paginated response to identify stale accounts that should be evaluated for deletion.

## Weekly scan setup

Starts the weekly stale guest discovery run and loads configuration values for SharePoint and Teams destinations.

value.ErrorDetails: Manual Action Required — automated deletion failed. Check Azure AD portal.

parameters.content:
## Entra ID Guest Account Governance: Automated Stale Account Detection, Sponsor Alerts, and Deletion

### How it works

This workflow governs Microsoft Entra ID guest accounts using two scheduled paths. A weekly process finds stale guest users, checks whether they are already queued in SharePoint, queues new pending deletions, and alerts either the sponsor or IT Security in Teams. A daily process reviews overdue pending deletions, honors retention exceptions, deletes eligible guest accounts, and records success, retention, or errors back to SharePoint and Teams.

### Setup steps

- Configure Microsoft Graph, Microsoft Entra ID, Microsoft SharePoint, Microsoft Teams, and Microsoft Graph Security credentials with permissions to read users, delete guest accounts, read/write SharePoint lists, and post Teams messages.
- Update the Config nodes with the correct SharePoint site ID, pending deletions list ID, audit log list ID, retention exceptions list ID, Teams team ID, and Teams channel ID.
- Create or verify SharePoint lists for pending deletions, audit logs, and retention exceptions with fields matching the workflow expressions, such as guest user ID, sponsor email, due date, status, and error details.
- Set the weekly and daily cron schedules to match the organization’s governance cadence and review the stale-account threshold used in the filtering code.
- Confirm Teams channels and sponsor-routing logic are appropriate before enabling account deletion in production.

### Customization

Adjust the stale-account age threshold, grace period before deletion, sponsor validation rules, Teams notification text, SharePoint list schema, and retention-exception criteria to match internal governance policy.

## Entra ID Guest Account Governance: Automated Stale Account Detection, Sponsor Alerts, and Deletion

### How it works

This workflow governs Microsoft Entra ID guest accounts using two scheduled paths. A weekly process finds stale guest users, checks whether they are already queued in SharePoint, queues new pending deletions, and alerts either the sponsor or IT Security in Teams. A daily process reviews overdue pending deletions, honors retention exceptions, deletes eligible guest accounts, and records success, retention, or errors back to SharePoint and Teams.

### Setup steps

- Configure Microsoft Graph, Microsoft Entra ID, Microsoft SharePoint, Microsoft Teams, and Microsoft Graph Security credentials with permissions to read users, delete guest accounts, read/write SharePoint lists, and post Teams messages.
- Update the Config nodes with the correct SharePoint site ID, pending deletions list ID, audit log list ID, retention exceptions list ID, Teams team ID, and Teams channel ID.
- Create or verify SharePoint lists for pending deletions, audit logs, and retention exceptions with fields matching the workflow expressions, such as guest user ID, sponsor email, due date, status, and error details.
- Set the weekly and daily cron schedules to match the organization’s governance cadence and review the stale-account threshold used in the filtering code.
- Confirm Teams channels and sponsor-routing logic are appropriate before enabling account deletion in production.

### Customization

Adjust the stale-account age threshold, grace period before deletion, sponsor validation rules, Teams notification text, SharePoint list schema, and retention-exception criteria to match internal governance policy.

## Weekly scan setup

Starts the weekly stale guest discovery run and loads configuration values for SharePoint and Teams destinations.

## Find stale guests

Retrieves guest users from Microsoft Graph and filters each paginated response to identify stale accounts that should be evaluated for deletion.

## Queue new candidates

Checks the SharePoint pending-deletions list to avoid duplicates, queues newly identified stale guests, and skips accounts that are already pending.

## Notify sponsor or security

Validates whether a queued account has a usable sponsor email, then posts either a sponsor notification or an IT Security alert to Teams.

## Daily execution setup

Starts the daily deletion execution run and loads configuration for pending deletions, audit logging, and retention exceptions.

## Collect overdue deletions

Gets overdue pending-deletion records from SharePoint, checks whether any are due, splits due records for item-by-item processing, or ends when nothing is due.

## Check retention exceptions

Looks up each overdue guest in the retention-exceptions list and branches based on whether deletion should be blocked.

## Delete or retain account

Handles the closely placed deletion and retention outcomes: eligible guests are deleted in Entra ID and checked for success, while exception-matched records are marked retained and logged to Teams.

## Record deletion outcomes

Records successful deletions to the SharePoint audit list and marks pending records deleted, while failed deletions generate a Teams alert and mark the pending item as errored.
